steps.title: Inspect PK concentration input
steps.concept: One-compartment oral PK concentration C(t) is the upstream physiological driver for the front end.
steps.student_question: How do absorption and elimination rates shape the concentration waveform that the sensor must track?
steps.expected_reasoning: Use time-domain transient simulation of PK-driven sensor current, first-order membrane lag, TIA response, and ADC quantization/saturation behavior reasoning around components [SRC_PK] and nodes [n_concentration_C]. Explain the physical quantity before computing it.
steps.reveal_hint: Name the component, node, or physical quantity first; then relate it to KCL/KVL, impedance, or energy storage.
steps.common_mistake: Jumping to a numeric answer before checking polarity, units, or the measured node.
steps.optional_value_reference: concentration C(t), lagged concentration after membrane mass transfer, sensor current, TIA output voltage, ADC output code, settling error
fact_lines: Components: SRC_PK
Nodes: n_concentration_C
Known values: SRC_PK (one-compartment oral PK concentration profile C(t)) uses uM units; generated PK defaults: peak scale 10 uM, ka 0.02 1/s, ke 0.002 1/s, bioavailability 1
Model values: SRC PK time; SRC PK one compartment oral PK C uM; probe concentration C uM
Measured outputs: concentration C(t), lagged concentration after membrane mass transfer, sensor current, TIA output voltage, ADC output code, settling error

steps.title: Inspect membrane mass-transfer lag
steps.concept: The first-order lag maps plasma/oral PK concentration to local membrane concentration and creates dynamic tracking error.
steps.student_question: What happens to peak timing and settling error when tau_m is increased or decreased?
steps.expected_reasoning: Use time-domain transient simulation of PK-driven sensor current, first-order membrane lag, TIA response, and ADC quantization/saturation behavior reasoning around components [LAG_M] and nodes [n_concentration_C, n_concentration_lagged]. Explain the physical quantity before computing it.
steps.reveal_hint: Name the component, node, or physical quantity first; then relate it to KCL/KVL, impedance, or energy storage.
steps.common_mistake: Jumping to a numeric answer before checking polarity, units, or the measured node.
steps.optional_value_reference: concentration C(t), lagged concentration after membrane mass transfer, sensor current, TIA output voltage, ADC output code, settling error
fact_lines: Components: LAG_M
Nodes: n_concentration_C, n_concentration_lagged
Known values: LAG_M (membrane mass-transfer lag tau_m) = 4 s
Model values: LAG M membrane tau m; probe lagged concentration uM
Measured outputs: concentration C(t), lagged concentration after membrane mass transfer, sensor current, TIA output voltage, ADC output code, settling error

steps.title: Inspect concentration-to-current conversion
steps.concept: The sensitivity gain creates the commanded faradaic current that drives the physical Simscape current source.
steps.student_question: For a given concentration range, what current range must the analog front end handle?
steps.expected_reasoning: Use time-domain transient simulation of PK-driven sensor current, first-order membrane lag, TIA response, and ADC quantization/saturation behavior reasoning around components [GAIN_SENS, I_SENSOR] and nodes [n_concentration_lagged, n_sensor_current_cmd, n_tia_sum]. Explain the physical quantity before computing it.
steps.reveal_hint: Name the component, node, or physical quantity first; then relate it to KCL/KVL, impedance, or energy storage.
steps.common_mistake: Jumping to a numeric answer before checking polarity, units, or the measured node.
steps.optional_value_reference: concentration C(t), lagged concentration after membrane mass transfer, sensor current, TIA output voltage, ADC output code, settling error
fact_lines: Components: GAIN_SENS, I_SENSOR
Nodes: n_concentration_lagged, n_sensor_current_cmd, n_tia_sum
Known values: GAIN_SENS (electrochemical sensor sensitivity) = 25 nA/uM; I_SENSOR (biosensor faradaic current source driven by lagged concentration) uses A units
Model values: GAIN SENS 25 nA per uM; I SENSOR current command A to PS; I SENSOR biosensor faradaic current; I SENSOR current probe; Sensor current A to Simulink; +1 more
Measured outputs: concentration C(t), lagged concentration after membrane mass transfer, sensor current, TIA output voltage, ADC output code, settling error

steps.title: Inspect TIA feedback network
steps.concept: Rf and Cf form the transimpedance feedback path around the band-limited op amp.
steps.student_question: How do Rf and Cf trade output scale, bandwidth, settling time, and noise filtering?
steps.expected_reasoning: Use time-domain transient simulation of PK-driven sensor current, first-order membrane lag, TIA response, and ADC quantization/saturation behavior reasoning around components [U_TIA, RF, CF] and nodes [n_tia_sum, n_tia_out]. Explain the physical quantity before computing it.
steps.reveal_hint: Name the component, node, or physical quantity first; then relate it to KCL/KVL, impedance, or energy storage.
steps.common_mistake: Jumping to a numeric answer before checking polarity, units, or the measured node.
steps.optional_value_reference: concentration C(t), lagged concentration after membrane mass transfer, sensor current, TIA output voltage, ADC output code, settling error
fact_lines: Components: U_TIA, RF, CF
Nodes: n_tia_sum, n_tia_out

Known values: U_TIA (transimpedance amplifier op amp) is parameterized; RF (TIA feedback resistor Rf) = 1 MOhm; CF (TIA feedback capacitor Cf) = 10 nF; generated op-amp defaults: rails 0 to 3.3 V, open-loop gain 1e5, pole 100 kHz
Model values: U TIA band limited op amp; RF TIA feedback 1Mohm; CF TIA feedback 10 nF; TIA output voltage sensor; TIA output V to Simulink
Measured outputs: concentration C(t), lagged concentration after membrane mass transfer, sensor current, TIA output voltage, ADC output code, settling error

steps.title: Inspect ADC quantization and saturation
steps.concept: The TIA output is saturated to the named ADC input range and quantized to a 12-bit code.
steps.student_question: What concentration or current level first causes ADC saturation for the chosen reference range?
steps.expected_reasoning: Use time-domain transient simulation of PK-driven sensor current, first-order membrane lag, TIA response, and ADC quantization/saturation behavior reasoning around components [ADC1] and nodes [n_tia_out, n_adc_in, n_adc_code]. Explain the physical quantity before computing it.

fact_lines: Components: ADC1
Nodes: n_tia_out, n_adc_in, n_adc_code
Known values: ADC1 (12-bit ADC) = 12 bits; generated ADC references: Vref lo 0 V, Vref hi 3.3 V
Model values: ADC1 input range saturation; ADC1 shift by vref lo; ADC1 volts to code scale; ADC1 12 bit quantizer; probe adc code
Measured outputs: concentration C(t), lagged concentration after membrane mass transfer, sensor current, TIA output voltage, ADC output code, settling error

steps.title: Inspect settling error probe
steps.concept: The settling-error path compares measured TIA output against the Rf-based steady-state estimate from the sensor current command.
steps.student_question: Which dynamic element dominates settling error: membrane transport or TIA bandwidth?
steps.expected_reasoning: Use time-domain transient simulation of PK-driven sensor current, first-order membrane lag, TIA response, and ADC quantization/saturation behavior reasoning around components [LAG_M, U_TIA, RF, CF] and nodes [n_concentration_C, n_concentration_lagged, n_tia_out]. Explain the physical quantity before computing it.

fact_lines: Components: LAG_M, U_TIA, RF, CF
Nodes: n_concentration_C, n_concentration_lagged, n_tia_out

Known values: LAG_M (membrane mass-transfer lag tau_m) = 4 s; U_TIA (transimpedance amplifier op amp) is parameterized; RF (TIA feedback resistor Rf) = 1 MOhm; CF (TIA feedback capacitor Cf) = 10 nF; generated op-amp defaults: rails 0 to 3.3 V, open-loop gain 1e5, pole 100 kHz
Model values: Expected TIA steady state from current; Expected TIA add reference; SETTLING ERROR Vout minus expected; probe settling error V
Measured outputs: concentration C(t), lagged concentration after membrane mass transfer, sensor current, TIA output voltage, ADC output code, settling error

steps.concept: Highlights that the biosensor current follows local membrane concentration rather than upstream concentration instantly.
